description: Write a README.md covering project overview, prerequisites, setup instructions, running locally, architecture notes, and demo.
design: Sections: Overview, Prerequisites (Python 3.11+, Node 18+), Setup (clone, venv, npm install), Running (flask run + npm start), Architecture (text diagram of React->Flask->JSON), Demo (screenshot placeholder).
title: README.md

description: Write unit tests for backend (Flask routes, controller methods) and frontend (React components, ApiClient). Backend: pytest. Frontend: Jest + React Testing Library.
design: Backend: tests/unit/test_hello_controller.py, test_app.py. Mock Flask context. Frontend: src/__tests__/HelloMessage.test.tsx, ApiClient.test.ts. Mock fetch. Coverage target: 80%.
title: Unit tests

description: Write E2E tests using Playwright that launch both backend and frontend and verify the Hello World message appears in the browser.
design: Playwright (Python or TypeScript). Test: navigate to localhost:3000, assert HelloMessage component visible with correct text. Setup fixture starts Flask and React dev server. Teardown stops servers.
title: End-to-end UI tests

description: Comprehensive test suite covering unit, integration, and end-to-end UI tests for both backend and frontend.
design: Backend: pytest for unit and integration. Frontend: Jest/React Testing Library for unit. Playwright or Cypress for E2E UI tests.
title: Testing

description: Implement a TypeScript ApiClient class that fetches the Hello World message from the Flask backend. Handle loading, success, and error states in the UI.
design: Class ApiClient: static async getHello(): Promise<HelloResponse>. Uses fetch() with base URL from env. HelloResponse interface: {message: string, status: string}. Error boundary or try/catch in component.
title: API client and backend integration

description: Write integration tests that verify the backend API endpoints respond correctly end-to-end through Flask's test client.
design: pytest with Flask test client (app.test_client()). Tests: GET /api/hello returns 200 with correct JSON body, GET /api/health returns 200. No mocking — real app stack.
title: Integration tests

description:
You are a senior software engineer and technical project manager. I want to build the simplest possible web application that displays a message (e.g., "Hello World").

Stack:
	•	Backend: Python + Flask
	•	Frontend: TypeScript + React
Phase 1 — Planning (do this now): 
	•	Create a structured epic with parent tickets and sub-tickets in the following format:
		⁃	[EPIC-#] Title [TICKET-#] Title (parent) [TICKET-#.1] Title (sub-ticket)
	•	Epics and tickets should have a hierarchical child parent relationships
	•	All tickets should include, description, design and acceptance information

The epic must include tickets for:
	•	Project setup & configuration
	•	Backend implementation (OOP, code comments required)
	•	Frontend implementation (OOP where applicable, code comments required)
	•	Unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end UI tests (as separate sub-tickets)
	•	A TESTING.md file with full testing instructions
	•	A README.md with setup, usage, demo instructions, and architecture notes

Phase 2 — Implementation (wait for my go-ahead):
	•	Implement one parent ticket at a time
	•	After completing each sub-ticket, add implementation notes directly to that ticket
	•	After finishing a parent ticket and all its test sub-tickets, stop and wait for me to create a pull request and share it before proceeding

Do not write any code yet. Only output the epic and tickets.
notes:
## Implementation Plan

[EPIC] beads3-5mo — Hello webapp prompt

[beads3-7nl] Project Setup & Configuration
  [beads3-s5a] Repository structure and virtual environment
  [beads3-j96] Flask backend configuration
  [beads3-pqq] React/TypeScript frontend configuration

[beads3-9ck] Backend Implementation
  [beads3-ogs] Flask app structure (OOP)
  [beads3-xp2] API endpoints

[beads3-z3t] Frontend Implementation
  [beads3-m45] React component structure
  [beads3-40i] API client and backend integration

[beads3-3i0] Testing
  [beads3-1gv] Unit tests
  [beads3-46p] Integration tests
  [beads3-2he] End-to-end UI tests

[beads3-jai] Documentation
  [beads3-9qs] TESTING.md
  [beads3-0ie] README.md

## Workflow
- Implement one parent ticket at a time
- Add implementation notes to each sub-ticket after completion
- Stop after each parent ticket (+ its tests) and wait for PR before proceeding
title: Hello webapp prompt

description: Set up the monorepo structure, virtual environment, and tooling for both backend and frontend.
design: Monorepo with /backend (Flask) and /frontend (React/TS) directories. Python venv in backend/.venv. Node modules in frontend/node_modules. Shared .gitignore.
title: Project Setup & Configuration

description: Implement the Flask backend using OOP principles with code comments throughout. Exposes a REST API serving the Hello World message.
design: Single Flask app class with route registration. OOP: App class wraps Flask instance, separate route handler classes. Code comments required on all methods and modules.
title: Backend Implementation

description: Write a TESTING.md file with full instructions for running all test suites: unit, integration, and E2E.
design: Sections: Prerequisites, Running Backend Unit Tests, Running Backend Integration Tests, Running Frontend Unit Tests, Running E2E Tests, CI notes. All commands copy-pasteable.
title: TESTING.md

description: Configure Flask app factory, environment variables (.env), CORS settings, and requirements.txt with pinned dependencies.
design: flask, flask-cors, python-dotenv in requirements.txt. App factory pattern: create_app() function. .env for PORT and DEBUG. CORS enabled for localhost:3000.
title: Flask backend configuration

description: Write TESTING.md with full testing instructions and README.md with setup, usage, demo, and architecture notes.
design: TESTING.md: step-by-step for running each test suite. README.md: prerequisites, setup, running locally, architecture diagram (text), demo screenshots section.
title: Documentation

description: Implement React components using TypeScript. App component as root, HelloMessage component displays the fetched message with loading and error states.
design: components/HelloMessage.tsx: functional component, accepts message prop. App.tsx: root component, manages fetch state. Inline comments on each component describing its role and props.
title: React component structure

description: Implement the Flask application using OOP design. App class encapsulates Flask instance and route registration. HelloController class handles the hello endpoint.
design: Class App: __init__ creates Flask instance, registers blueprints. Class HelloController: static method hello() returns JSON {message: 'Hello World'}. Module-level docstrings and method comments.
title: Flask app structure (OOP)

description: Configure TypeScript, ESLint, and package.json scripts. Set up proxy to backend for local development.
design: tsconfig.json strict mode. ESLint with TypeScript rules. package.json proxy field pointing to http://localhost:5000. Scripts: start, build, test.
title: React/TypeScript frontend configuration

description: Initialize monorepo directory layout, Python virtual environment, and Node.js project. Add .gitignore covering .venv, node_modules, __pycache__, .env, dist, build.
design: Directories: /backend, /frontend, /tests. backend/.venv via python3 -m venv. frontend via create-react-app --template typescript or Vite. Root .gitignore.
title: Repository structure and virtual environment

description: Define and implement REST API endpoints. GET /api/hello returns Hello World JSON. GET /api/health returns service health status.
design: Blueprint 'api' registered on App. Routes: GET /api/hello -> {message, status}, GET /api/health -> {status: ok}. Response dataclass or TypedDict. JSON content-type headers.
title: API endpoints

description: Implement the React/TypeScript frontend that fetches and displays the Hello World message from the backend API.
design: React functional components with TypeScript. OOP where applicable (API client class). Code comments required. Fetches from Flask backend on mount.
title: Frontend Implementation
